title: Decoding instruction 219 (11 01 10 11) into its four 2-bit fields
setup:
# --- plot helper (hidden) — the decode ruler, driven by the student's getters --
# Splits one instruction word into its four fields using the student's decode
# functions and draws a bar per field. Each falls back to a plain shift so the
# panel never blanks.
def __bar(x, height):
    left = x + 0.15
    right = x + 0.85
    return [[left, 0], [left, height], [right, height], [right, 0]]

def __field(name, fallback, instr):
    f = globals().get(name)
    if f is not None:
        try:
            v = f(instr)
            if isinstance(v, int):
                return v
        except Exception:
            pass
    return fallback

def __plot(instr):
    op = __field("get_opcode", (instr >> 6) & 3, instr)
    dest = __field("get_dest", (instr >> 4) & 3, instr)
    srcA = __field("get_srcA", (instr >> 2) & 3, instr)
    srcB = __field("get_srcB", instr & 3, instr)
    return [
        {"name": "opcode", "points": __bar(0, op), "highlight": True},
        {"name": "dest", "points": __bar(1, dest), "highlight": True},
        {"name": "srcA", "points": __bar(2, srcA), "highlight": True},
        {"name": "srcB", "points": __bar(3, srcB), "highlight": True},
    ]
